data.narrative:
Nara Space Technology is a Series B space and aerospace company that has raised approximately $25M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 3 private-sector peers at identical stage and sector: Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M), alongside a broader reference set of 5 public Space & Aerospace equities including Rocket Lab and Intuitive Machines.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable approach. The three same-stage, same-sector private comparables yield a median valuation of $400M, with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (sector median: 53.2x across 6 tickers including RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue figures, which private companies typically withhold. The peer median therefore grounds the valuation estimate.

The triangulated valuation range is $340M to $460M, centered at $400M. This reflects the median peer comparable directly. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory and unit economics, absent from available data; capital efficiency relative to peers; and customer concentration risk in the early commercial-stage space sector. The wide spread in peer valuations ($274M–$1.3B) signals material dispersion based on technology differentiation and customer traction not captured in stage and sector alone.
